results.title: Connecting an AI agent to email (AI agents - Advanced only) – Zendesk help
results.content:
## Connecting an AI agent to your subdomain

The first step in connecting an AI agent to email is to connect to your production or
sandbox environment.

To connect an AI agent to your subdomain

This user appears as the sender only
when the ticket is viewed in the Agent Workspace. When a customer receives an AI agent
email response in their inbox, the sender appears as the brand name and support address you’ve selected
in Admin Center.

## Creating the automation trigger and turning on the automation engine

After you connect to your subdomain, you need to create the automation trigger and turn on
the automation engine. This allows the AI agent to send replies to customer requests on the
email channel. [...] The trigger is automatically created.
You can view the details of the JSON in a read-only text window, and edit the trigger if needed.

Your AI agent is now connected to email. At this point, you may want to:

## Disconnecting an AI agent from email

If you no longer want an AI agent to be connected to email, you can turn off the automation
engine and deactivate the trigger.

At this point, the AI agent is
still technically connected to the email channel, but it won’t respond to customer
requests.

Your AI agent is no longer connected to email. [...] After youcreate an AI agent, you need to connect it to Zendesk Support before the AI agent can begin responding to customers by email. Email responses sent by the AI agent are sent as thesystemuser, meaning no agent seat is required for an email AI agent.

|  |  |
 --- |
| Add-on | AI agents - Advanced |

After you create an AI agent, you need to connect it to Zendesk
Support before the AI agent can begin responding to customers by email. Email responses sent
by the AI agent are sent as the system user, meaning no agent seat is required for an
email AI agent.

You should connect only one AI agent to email. For advice on how to handle brand-specific
information within a single AI agent, see Can I connect multiple AI agents to a single Zendesk account?

results.title: How to Handle Customer Complaints Automatically Using AI Agents
results.content:
Use Case:-

Whenever a customer submits a complaint through a form, an AI agent automatically reviews the complaint details and generates a personalized customer support email. The email acknowledges the issue, responds appropriately based on the complaint, and is sent directly to the customer. This helps me handle complaints faster, provide consistent support, and respond professionally without manual effort.

up, and a customer was frustrated. Normally, you’d spend hours digging through inboxes, chat transcripts, and spreadsheets. With Chat Logs? Effortless. Every chat your agents have ever handled holds lessons, patterns, and opportunities to improve.  But without a single place to track and analyze them, all that data just… disappears. Imagine you could:  • Review every conversation instantly.  • Train your AI on exactly what your customers are asking.  • Spot recurring issues before they become major headaches.  • Empower your team to deliver smarter, faster support every single time. That’s what 𝗕𝗼𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗰 𝗖𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗟𝗼𝗴𝘀 does. Not just storing conversations. Turning them into actionable intelligence. Here’s a real scenario: Your AI handled 1,000 queries last week. results.title: Under the Hood of USPS: Automation, Innovation and AI
results.content: sort a million parcels a day. So, as a plant manager in Harrisburg, I don't think I sorted a million parcels in a week. So, to have a facility with this type of volume, you do not want it to go down at any point due to a breakage or a failure. We have predictive windows where we have maintenance take care of the machine every day, but you don't want that machine to go down at any other time. So, what we're seeing with AI is that we can monitor vibrations, we can monitor sound, we can monitor temperature, and we can feed that information into a model so that we can become predictive as to what part needs to be changed before we suffer a breakage. What we also have, and this has been around for a little bit, is in our customer call centers. We have an AI agent, we call her Ava, and Ava's
